_note: Phase 4 OOD HEADROOM PROBE (design doc docs/phase4_rl_design.md sec 8). The Goal-specialized RL anchor pi_0 (= rl_base_16k, the 16k SFT that scores 89.0% on LIBERO-Goal) is evaluated ZERO-SHOT on libero_10 (LIBERO-Long) to answer the sec-8 question: is there an unsaturated suite where an RL effect could be visible, or does filtered/reward-weighted BC STARVE there (no successful rollouts to learn from)? Method = collection==eval (rl_rollout.py --suite libero_10): each rollout's terminal binary success IS the per-task pc_success, validated as Phase-4 smoke gate 2. Raw per-task kept-rollout .pt on Drive: VLATune/rl/probe_libero10_pi0/rollouts_task{tid}.pt (a tiny ~1.3KB pickle = empty kept-list when 0 successes). THIS IS A PROBE, NOT A CLAIM (n=10/task, single policy, 5-of-10 tasks); it answers feasibility, not effect size.
status: CLOSED (Phase 4, 2026-06-17). The starvation conclusion was DECIDED by tasks 0,1 = 0/10 each -- a binary feasibility question (can filtered/reward-weighted BC bootstrap on libero_10? -> NO: zero successful rollouts to filter), for which effect-size precision is unnecessary once two independent tasks score 0/10. tasks 2-4 and pi_1 were therefore NOT run to completion; their per-task entries are left as not_finalized rather than fabricated. Superseded by Phase 5 Track B (demo-seed libero_10), which SFTs pi_0 off 0% so the filter no longer starves.
method: Zero-shot transfer eval of a goal-only policy on an OOD suite, via the rollout collector (collection==eval). For each libero_10 task, run n rollouts of pi_0 under the EXACT eval contract (relative control, n_action_steps=10, camera rename_map image->camera1/image2->camera2, MUJOCO_GL=egl, paired seeds from 1000); the fraction whose terminal LIBERO goal-predicate fires within the step limit is pc_success. No fine-tuning is run on libero_10 -- the probe's purpose is to measure whether filtered BC COULD bootstrap there at all.

policies.pi_0: rl_base_16k/pretrained_model = the pinned Phase-4 anchor = smolvla_sft_goal_run1 `last` @ step 16000 (89.0% n=100 on LIBERO-Goal; results/sft_libero_goal.json). Goal-suite-only SFT of lerobot/smolvla_base.
policies.pi_1_not_probed: rl_iter1_b10/checkpoints/001000 (the overall-best Goal policy, 91.0%). NOT probed on libero_10: pi_1 = pi_0 fine-tuned on pi_0's *Goal* successful rollouts, so it carries no libero_10 skill and is expected to be ~identically near-zero on this OOD suite. A 1-task spot-check could confirm but is a foregone ~0 at ~24 min/task of slow timeout episodes.

suite_note: LIBERO-Long: 10 long-horizon, multi-step tasks. OUT-OF-DISTRIBUTION for a libero_goal-only policy (different tasks/scenes/instructions and a far longer horizon). Reproduced range in the literature ~52-71% for in-distribution training; near-zero-shot for our goal-only checkpoint.

eval_protocol.kind: collection == eval (rollout binary success == lerobot pc_success, Phase-4 smoke gate 2)
eval_protocol.tasks_probed_note: 5 of 10 libero_10 tasks in ONE process (20 envs); building all 10 at n_envs=4 = 40 envs risks a host-RAM OOM (Phase-4 ops note). A second process would cover 5-9 if warranted -- not warranted given tasks 0,1 = 0/10.
eval_protocol.seeds: paired, deterministic from batch index starting 1000

headline: STARVATION CONFIRMED (sec 8 caveat). The goal-only RL anchor transfers to LIBERO-Long at ~0%: task0 = 0/10 and task1 = 0/10 (task2 trending 0/8). With zero successful rollouts there is NOTHING for filtered/reward-weighted BC to filter or imitate, so the rollout->filter->fine-tune loop CANNOT bootstrap on libero_10. The unsaturated suite that could have shown an RL effect is too far OOD for this method to start.
interpretation: This is the exact failure mode design sec 8 flagged: 'filtered BC needs SOME successes to bootstrap -- if the success rate is very low, the filter starves (sparse reward bites hardest exactly where there is the most headroom).' A goal-only SmolVLA never completes a long-horizon libero_10 task within the step limit, so the method has no signal to learn from. Combined with the Goal result (results/rl_libero_goal.json: RL-lite is a within-noise REDISTRIBUTION on the saturated in-distribution suite, no durable aggregate gain), Phase 4 yields a clean, honest DUAL outcome: (1) on saturated in-distribution Goal, RL-lite produces no measurable headroom; (2) on unsaturated OOD libero_10, the policy is too far below threshold for filtered BC to even begin. Neither is an RL gain -- and both are legitimate, defensible findings, consistent with PROJECT_EXPLAINER's stance that an honest null/negative is a respectable deliverable.
corroborating_evidence: Slowness IS signal here: every libero_10 episode ran to the FULL long-horizon timeout (no early success termination), so a batch of 4 took ~8 min vs ~2-2.5 min for Goal. Near-total failure is the cause of the slowness, independently corroborating the 0% read.
confounds: Probe-grade, not claim-grade: n=10/task, 5 of 10 tasks, single policy (pi_0). But 0/10 on two independent tasks is decisive for the binary feasibility question (can filtered BC bootstrap? -> no); effect-size precision is not needed to conclude starvation.
Only pi_0 probed; pi_1 (Goal-fine-tuned) expected ~identical on OOD libero_10 (no libero_10 skill added by Goal fine-tuning).
pi_0 is the 16k (under-trained vs 20k) SFT -- irrelevant here: a fully-trained goal-only policy would also be ~0 zero-shot on LIBERO-Long.
Sim-only; outcome-only binary success; goal-only base by design.
decision: Do NOT run the rollout->filter->fine-tune loop on libero_10 from this checkpoint -- it starves (sec 8). The honest deliverable is this descriptive OOD null. Phase 4's reportable result is the methodology + the dual null (Goal redistribution + libero_10 starvation), not an RL improvement.
what_would_make_rl_measurable: DEMO-SEED libero_10: first SFT pi_0 on a small number of libero_10 demonstrations to lift it off 0% (give it nonzero success to bootstrap), THEN apply the rollout->filter->fine-tune loop. This is the sec-8 'demo-seed' fallback and the most direct route to a measurable RL effect on an unsaturated suite.

A less-extreme OOD probe with nonzero base success: held-out Goal tasks (train RL on 8, eval on 2) or perturbed initial states -- in-distribution-ish OOD where the policy already succeeds sometimes, so the filter does not starve.
Where measurable, claim-grade rigor (design sec 10): n=500 eval episodes (50/task x 10), >=3 seeds varying both RL-train and eval seeds, report mean +/- std, confounds stated up front.
wall_time_note: Single L4, ~8 min per batch of 4 (every episode runs to the long-horizon timeout) -- ~23 min/task at n=10. Background execution plus per-task .pt checkpointing to Drive made the run robust to a disconnected monitoring session.
